aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/Neighbor.title: Neighbor
aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/Neighbor.description: since | 1.0.0 --- | --- A neighbor node used in a proof. See [Proof](#Proof) for details.

aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/ProofStep.title: ProofStep
aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/ProofStep.description: since | 1.0.0 --- | --- We distinguish three kind of proof steps: Branch, Fork and Leaf. Each step contains a `skip` value which corresponds to the length of the common prefix at that particular level. The details of each level is documented [in the wiki :: Proof Format](https://github.com/aiken-lang/merkle-patricia-forestry/wiki/Proof-format).

aiken_design_patterns/linked_list/Elemen.title: ElementData
aiken_design_patterns/linked_list/Elemen.description:
Similar to [`Element`](#Element) above, the API is designed so that you
 wouldn't need to work with this datatype directly.

 Continuations that provide element data implicitly signal whether it should
 be coerced into a `root_data` or a `node_data` through the (potential) key.
 That is, if no key is provided, it means the element at hand is a root
 element.

bifrost/types/spo_bans/BanListDatum.title: BanListDatum
bifrost/types/spo_bans/BanListDatum.description:
The terminology used here is as follows:
 - `Root` is the first element of the linked list
 - `Node` is any other element in a linked list other than the root
 - `Element` is the umbrella term to refer to either the root or nodes

 You probably won't need to interact with this.

cardano/transaction/OutputReference.title: OutputReference
cardano/transaction/OutputReference.description:
An `OutputReference` is a unique reference to an output on-chain. The `output_index`
 corresponds to the position in the output list of the transaction (identified by its id)
 that produced that output
